title: senecio pugioniformis
intro: Senecio pugioniformis, also known as the needle spurge or needle bush, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Senecio pugioniformis include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its possible antifungal and antibacterial effects. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal and health benefits.
